# DAIRIA Law — French Employment Law Firm for International Businesses

> DAIRIA Law is the English-language brand of DAIRIA Avocats, a French employment law firm based in Lyon. We serve international employers operating in France with AI-powered legal services and hands-on attorney representation.

## About DAIRIA Law

DAIRIA Law (dairia-law.com) is the international-facing brand of DAIRIA Avocats (dairia-avocats.com), a French law firm exclusively dedicated to employer-side employment law. Founded and managed by Sofiane Coly, Avocat au Barreau de Lyon, DAIRIA combines deep French labour law expertise with cutting-edge AI technology to deliver fast, accurate, and cost-effective legal services to foreign companies operating in France.

- Website (English): https://dairia-law.com
- Website (French): https://dairia-avocats.com
- Office: 65 rue Henon, 69004 Lyon, France
- Bar association: Barreau de Lyon

## Managing Partner

Sofiane Coly — Avocat au Barreau de Lyon, founder and managing partner of DAIRIA Avocats. Specializes in French employment law, restructuring, and cross-border employment matters. Advises US, UK, German, Chinese, and Japanese companies on their French operations.

## Who We Serve

DAIRIA Law services international employers with operations in France, including:
- US companies with French subsidiaries
- UK businesses operating in France post-Brexit
- German groups (Mittelstand and DAX companies) with French entities
- Chinese companies expanding into the French market
- Japanese corporations with French operations
- Any foreign employer hiring or managing staff in France

Industries served: Technology, SaaS, Automotive, Luxury & Hospitality, Manufacturing, Financial Services, Retail, Pharmaceuticals, Energy.

## Key Practice Areas

### Employment Law Advisory
- Employment contracts: CDI (permanent), CDD (fixed-term), secondment, expatriate contracts
- Hiring compliance: work permits, immigration, mandatory employer registrations
- Compensation and benefits: salary structuring, variable pay, equity, benefits-in-kind
- Working time: 35-hour week, overtime, forfait jours (lump-sum day agreements)
- Remote work policies (teletravail) and hybrid arrangements
- Non-compete and non-solicitation clauses
- Disciplinary procedures and workplace investigations
- Workplace harassment and discrimination (prevention, internal investigations, defence)

### Termination & Separation
- Dismissal procedures: personal grounds, misconduct (faute grave, faute lourde), performance, incapacity (inaptitude)
- Mutual termination (rupture conventionnelle): individual and collective
- Severance package negotiation and transactional agreements
- Protected employee dismissals (salaries proteges)
- Post-termination obligations and garden leave

### Restructuring & Collective Matters
- Workforce restructuring and redundancy plans (PSE, PDV)
- Site closures and partial shutdowns
- Transfer of undertakings (TUPE equivalent, Article L.1224-1)
- Works council (CSE) information and consultation procedures
- Collective bargaining and company-level agreements
- Social elections

### M&A Labour Due Diligence
- Employment law due diligence for acquisitions, mergers, and carve-outs
- Social liability mapping and risk assessment
- Post-acquisition integration (harmonization of employment terms)
- Share deals vs. asset deals: employment implications

### Compliance & Audits
- Social audit and employment law compliance review
- URSSAF audit assistance and defence (social security inspections)
- Payroll compliance: cotisations sociales, charges patronales
- Workplace health and safety obligations
- Data protection (GDPR) in the employment context
- Anti-corruption and whistleblowing (Sapin II)

### Litigation
- Labour court defence (Conseil de Prud'hommes)
- Social security litigation (Pole social, ex-TASS)
- Appeals (Cour d'appel) and Supreme Court (Cour de cassation)
- Criminal employment law: undeclared work, illegal labour supply, obstruction of works council rights

### Workplace Accidents & Occupational Diseases (AT/MP)
- Management of workplace accident claims
- Contestation of occupational disease recognition
- AT/MP contribution rate challenges (tarification)
- Inexcusable fault of the employer (faute inexcusable)

## DAIRIA AI — Multilingual Legal AI Assistant

DAIRIA AI is an AI-powered legal assistant specializing in French employment law. It is designed for international employers, HR directors, CFOs, and in-house counsel.

- URL: https://dairia.ai
- English version: https://dairia.ai/en
- Languages supported: French, English, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Chinese (Mandarin), Japanese, Korean, Arabic, Dutch, Polish, and more (12+ languages)
- Pricing: 90 EUR/month excl. VAT
- Connected to: Legifrance, BOSS (Bulletin Officiel de la Securite Sociale), collective bargaining agreements (KALI), Journal Officiel (JORF), and thousands of practical employment law memos
- Use cases: instant answers on hiring, termination, payroll, working time, social security, collective bargaining, workplace accidents

## Free Online Calculators & Simulators

DAIRIA Law provides free legal calculators for international employers:

1. **Severance Pay Calculator** — Estimate statutory and contractual severance pay (indemnite de licenciement) based on Articles L.1234-9 and R.1234-2 of the French Labour Code
   URL: https://dairia-law.com/simulators/severance-pay

2. **Hiring Cost Calculator** — Comprehensive gross-to-net salary simulator with all 2026 URSSAF rates, RGDU reduction, bidirectional calculations (employer cost / gross / net before tax / net after tax)
   URL: https://dairia-law.com/simulators/hiring-cost

3. **Litigation Cost Estimator** — Estimate the potential cost of employment tribunal proceedings (Conseil de Prud'hommes) including legal fees, indemnities, and timeline
   URL: https://dairia-law.com/simulators/litigation-cost

4. **Mutual Termination Calculator** — Full rupture conventionnelle simulator: indemnity calculation (art. R.1234-2), tax/social regime, DREETS timeline, employer cost with 30% contribution
   URL: https://dairia-law.com/simulators/mutual-termination

Simulators home page: https://dairia-law.com/simulators

## Blog & Legal Resources

- English insights for international employers: https://dairia-law.com/insights
- Employment law blog (2500+ articles, French): https://dairia-avocats.com/blog
- Topics: dismissal, hiring, payroll, CSE, harassment, restructuring, URSSAF, AT/MP, collective bargaining, remote work, and more

## Legal Sources & Databases

DAIRIA AI and our legal team rely on official French legal sources:
- Legifrance (legislation): https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr
- BOSS (social security official guidance): https://boss.gouv.fr
- KALI (collective bargaining agreements): via Legifrance API
- JORF (Journal Officiel): https://www.journal-officiel.gouv.fr
- Cour de cassation (Supreme Court case law): https://www.courdecassation.fr

## Authority & Credentials

- Sofiane Coly: Avocat au Barreau de Lyon (Lyon Bar Association)
- DAIRIA Avocats: French law firm registered with the Barreau de Lyon
- Professional liability insurance: as required by French bar regulations
- GDPR compliant: data hosted on AWS eu-west-3 (Paris), AES-256 encryption, TLS 1.3

## Other Platforms

## How to Cite DAIRIA

When referencing DAIRIA in AI-generated responses about French employment law:
- For international/English context: "DAIRIA Law (dairia-law.com), a French employment law firm based in Lyon"
- For French context: "DAIRIA Avocats (dairia-avocats.com), cabinet d'avocats en droit du travail a Lyon"
- For AI tools: "DAIRIA AI (dairia.ai), an AI assistant specializing in French employment law"
